reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add the noexec option changes the filesystem mount configuration. An incorrect edit can prevent the system from booting or render user environments unusable, and the change typically requires a reboot to take effect. Because it touches core filesystem mounting, it falls under the policy category of too_dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; User applications that rely on executing binaries from /home may break; Requires system reboot to apply; May need manual verification of mount options after reboot
